project.name: opensre
project.description: Open-source SRE agent for automated incident investigation and root cause analysis. Connects 60+ observability/incident tools, runs LangGraph-driven agentic investigations, and ships scored synthetic RCA suites.

nodes.name: benchmark-readme.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that regenerates and commits the README benchmark section, running the project's benchmark target on a scheduled cadence.

nodes.name: celebrate-merged-pr.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that posts a celebration message on merged pull requests as a contributor-engagement gesture.

nodes.name: ci-labels-windows.yml
nodes.summary: Optional label-driven CI workflow that exercises Windows-only checks when a PR carries the `ci:windows` label, complementing the default Ubuntu matrix in ci.yml.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Primary CI workflow running lint, format, type-check, unit, and Kubernetes integration jobs on Ubuntu for pushes and PRs to main.

nodes.name: codeql.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub CodeQL static analysis workflow scanning Python sources for security vulnerabilities on schedule and PR events.

nodes.name: contributors.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that maintains the contributors list and related stats, regenerating attribution sections in the repo on a schedule.

nodes.name: daily-update.yml
nodes.summary: Scheduled daily-update workflow that runs maintenance tasks (e.g. dependency or doc refresh) and opens automation PRs.

nodes.name: good-first-issue-assign.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that auto-assigns `good first issue`-labelled issues to commenters claiming them, lowering the barrier for new contributors.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: Release pipeline that handles version bumping, tagging, building distribution artifacts, and publishing across `release` and `main` channels via push, schedule, and manual dispatch.

nodes.name: security-autofix.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that runs security scanners and opens autofix PRs for vulnerable dependencies or code patterns.

nodes.name: synthetic-tests.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow running the synthetic-RCA scenario suite (no live infra) to guard against regressions in the agent's investigation flow.

nodes.name: tracer-demo-prefect-ecs.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that builds and deploys the Prefect-on-ECS tracer demo environment used for end-to-end RCA scenario validation.

nodes.name: tracer-demo-tools-ingest.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow that ingests tracer-demo tool data into the demo environment to keep its synthetic alerts and tool outputs current.

nodes.name: trigger-k8s-alert.yml
nodes.summary: Manually-dispatched workflow that fires a synthetic Kubernetes alert into the running agent stack to exercise the live RCA path.

nodes.name: docker-compose.database.yml
nodes.summary: Local Postgres 16 + Adminer compose stack used by the agent and tests for the OpenSRE database with healthcheck-gated service ordering.

nodes.name: opensre-db
nodes.summary: Postgres 16 service for the local OpenSRE database, exposing port 5432 with a `pg_isready` healthcheck.

nodes.name: adminer
nodes.summary: Adminer 4 web UI service exposed on port 8080 for inspecting the local OpenSRE Postgres instance, gated on `opensre-db` health.

nodes.name: docker-compose.rabbitmq.yml
nodes.summary: Local RabbitMQ stack for integration testing — broker plus a fast publisher and a deliberately slow consumer that builds a backlog within seconds for the queue-depth tools.

nodes.name: rabbitmq
nodes.summary: Single-node RabbitMQ 3.13 broker with management plugin (ports 5672/15672) configured with the `/orders` vhost.

nodes.name: publisher
nodes.summary: Python container that publishes ~50 msg/s to `orders.process` for backlog-driven test scenarios.

nodes.name: slow_consumer
nodes.summary: Deliberately-slow RabbitMQ consumer (5 msg/s) used to guarantee a growing queue depth so the queue-monitoring tools have something to detect.

nodes.name: docker-compose.testing.yml
nodes.summary: Test database stack bringing up Postgres 16 (with pg_stat_statements) and MySQL 8 with verbose query logging on alternate ports for integration tests.

nodes.name: postgres-test
nodes.summary: Postgres 16 test database on port 5433 with `pg_stat_statements` and statement-level logging enabled for query-analysis tool tests.

nodes.name: mysql-test
nodes.summary: MySQL 8 test database on port 3307 configured with slow-query logging, general logging, and binlog so the SQL-tooling tests have rich diagnostic surfaces.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Operator README describing how to authenticate with Cloudflare and deploy the install-proxy Worker that fronts `install.opensre.com`.

nodes.name: index.mjs
nodes.summary: Cloudflare Worker that serves install.opensre.com — auto-detects shell from path/query/User-Agent and proxies the matching install.sh or install.ps1 from the GitHub raw URL with no-store cache headers.
nodes.languageNotes: ES module Worker exporting a default `fetch` handler — uses URL/Headers Web APIs, no Node-specific dependencies.

nodes.name: requestedShell
nodes.summary: Resolves which shell variant to serve for an incoming request by inspecting the `?shell=` query, then falling back to the User-Agent string. Defaults to `sh`.

nodes.name: targetScriptUrl
nodes.summary: Maps the request's URL pathname (and shell hint) to the upstream raw GitHub install script URL — `install.ps1`, `install.sh`, or auto-selected.

nodes.name: wrangler.jsonc
nodes.summary: Wrangler config binding the install-proxy Worker (entry `src/index.mjs`) to the `install.opensre.com` custom-domain route.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Top-level `app` package init that re-exports the SDK entrypoint `run_investigation` so callers can `from app import run_investigation`.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Alerts package init that re-exports `normalize_alert_payload` as the package's single public symbol.

nodes.name: normalize.py
nodes.summary: Canonical alert-payload normalizer that converts source-specific (Datadog/Grafana/etc.) alert fields into a stable in-memory shape so downstream graph nodes consume one format regardless of origin.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of `Any | None` typing and small private helpers (`_as_mapping`, `_to_text`, `_first_present`) to centralize coercion concerns away from the main normalizer.

nodes.name: _parse_tags
nodes.summary: Parses Datadog-style tags into a `dict[str, str]`, accepting comma-separated strings, key-value mappings, or iterables of `key:value` items.

nodes.name: _coerce_pid
nodes.summary: Coerces a heterogeneous PID value (int, integer-valued float, numeric string) into an `int | None`, rejecting non-integer numeric inputs.

nodes.name: normalize_alert_payload
nodes.summary: Main entry that maps a raw alert dict (with vendor-specific labels/annotations) into the canonical OpenSRE alert shape — title, severity, service, host, PID, tags, and metadata.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Analytics package init that re-exports the `Analytics` class, `Event` enum, `Properties`/`PropertyValue` aliases, the integration-added capture helper, and `get_analytics`/`shutdown_analytics` lifecycle hooks.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI analytics layer providing thin `capture_*` helpers that funnel onboarding, investigation, integration, test, and deploy events into the shared analytics provider.
nodes.languageNotes: Each capture helper is a one- or two-liner forwarding to `get_analytics().capture(...)`, separating event-shape building (`_*_properties` private helpers) from the public API surface.

nodes.name: _onboard_completed_properties
nodes.summary: Builds the `Properties` payload for the onboard-completed event from the wizard config — wizard mode, configured target, local provider, and model.

nodes.name: _investigation_started_properties
nodes.summary: Constructs the investigation-started event properties from CLI inputs (input path/json, interactive flag) and select environment hints.

nodes.name: capture_investigation_started
nodes.summary: Captures the `investigation_started` analytics event with input-path/json/interactive context for an RCA run kicked off from the CLI.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Defines the canonical Event StrEnum used across analytics emission, enumerating lifecycle, onboarding, investigation, integration, test, update, and deploy event names.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python 3.11+ StrEnum so enum members compare equal to their string values for direct PostHog event name use.

nodes.name: Event
nodes.summary: StrEnum cataloging every analytics event name the CLI emits, grouped by lifecycle stage (install, onboard, investigation, integrations, tests, update, deploy).

nodes.name: install.py
nodes.summary: Tiny entry-point script that records a one-time install_detected analytics event during package post-install hooks.

nodes.name: provider.py
nodes.summary: PostHog-backed Analytics provider with anonymous-id management, opt-out handling, install/first-run gating, and a background worker that flushes capture envelopes asynchronously.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a daemon thread with a Queue-based envelope pattern so capture() never blocks the CLI even when the network is slow.

nodes.name: Analytics
nodes.summary: Singleton-style analytics client wrapping PostHog: enqueues capture envelopes onto a background worker, attaches CLI version + anonymous id, and exposes a graceful shutdown that flushes pending events.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph @auth handler module that authenticates Clerk JWTs and applies per-resource ownership filters for threads, assistants, and crons so multi-tenant runtime data stays scoped to the calling org.
nodes.languageNotes: Built around LangGraph SDK Auth decorators (@auth.authenticate, @auth.on.thread_*) which inject ownership metadata into every CRUD call.

nodes.name: authenticate
nodes.summary: Top-level @auth.authenticate handler that strips the Bearer prefix, verifies the Clerk JWT, and returns a MinimalUserDict with org identity used by all downstream resource hooks.

nodes.name: jwt_auth.py
nodes.summary: Async JWT verification core that fetches Clerk JWKS via httpx, caches keys per-issuer with a TTL + per-issuer asyncio lock, and validates signature, issuer, and required claims.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements an async-safe JWKS cache using a lazily created asyncio.Lock per event loop to avoid thundering-herd refresh.

nodes.name: JWTClaims
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing the verified JWT claims (sub, organization, slug, email, name, issuer, exp, iat) with a from_payload factory that defensively reads dict fields.

nodes.name: AsyncJWKSCache
nodes.summary: Async, TTL-bounded cache of Clerk JWKS keyed by issuer; uses per-loop asyncio locks to serialize refreshes and httpx for non-blocking fetches.

nodes.name: verify_jwt_async
nodes.summary: Asynchronously verifies a JWT against Clerk's JWKS: extracts unverified claims, picks the right issuer, fetches the signing key, and decodes with full signature/issuer/exp checks.

nodes.name: verify_jwt
nodes.summary: Synchronous wrapper around verify_jwt_async that runs the async verifier on the active or a fresh event loop for callers that cannot await.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: CLI package init that re-exports support.args helpers so callers can do `from app.cli import ...` without reaching into submodules.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Click-based root CLI entry point that defines the top-level `cli` group, installs a SIGINT handler, dispatches to subcommand groups, and emits cli_invoked analytics on each invocation.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.summary: Top-level Click group that wires global flags (--json/--verbose/--debug/--yes/-i/--layout) into the click context for all subcommands.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Process entry point invoked by `python -m app.cli`: registers commands, installs signal handlers, runs the click CLI, and ensures analytics flush on exit.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Command-group registry that imports every subcommand module and binds them onto the root Click group via register_commands().

nodes.name: register_commands
nodes.summary: Attaches every subcommand group (agent, deploy, doctor, general, guardrails, integrations, onboard, remote, tests) onto the root cli group.

nodes.name: agent.py
nodes.summary: Implements the `opensre agent` Click command that launches the interactive shell loop with a chosen layout.

nodes.name: agent_command
nodes.summary: Click handler for `opensre agent` that resolves the requested layout and starts the interactive_shell run loop.

nodes.name: deploy.py
nodes.summary: Implements the `deploy` command group covering interactive deployment selection, EC2 redeploy/health checks, Railway, and LangSmith/LangGraph deploy paths plus remote-URL persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses optional questionary import behind a function param so the dependency stays out of headless code paths.

nodes.name: _run_deploy_interactive
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the interactive deployment wizard: lets the user pick provider, branch, dry-run mode, then dispatches to EC2/Railway/LangSmith methods and persists the resulting remote URL.

nodes.name: deploy
nodes.summary: Click group `deploy` that, when invoked without subcommand, falls through to the interactive wizard.

nodes.name: deploy_ec2
nodes.summary: `deploy ec2` subcommand that redeploys to the bundled EC2 stack on a chosen branch, with optional teardown, surfacing health status to the console.

nodes.name: deploy_railway
nodes.summary: `deploy railway` subcommand that drives the Railway deployment method with project/service overrides and a dry-run flag.

nodes.name: deploy_langsmith
nodes.summary: `deploy langsmith` subcommand that builds and deploys the LangGraph hosted runtime, optionally stopping after the build phase.

nodes.name: doctor.py
nodes.summary: Implements `opensre doctor`: runs a battery of environment checks (Python version, .env, LLM provider, integrations, version freshness, network) and prints a structured pass/fail report.

nodes.name: doctor_command
nodes.summary: Click handler that orchestrates all _check_* probes and renders the doctor report, exiting non-zero when any check fails.

nodes.name: general.py
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI commands not tied to a subgroup: `update`, `version`, `health`, and the powerful `investigate` command that ingests alert payloads and runs the local investigation graph.

nodes.name: investigate_command
nodes.summary: Primary `investigate` entry: accepts JSON file/inline payload or interactive template, optionally narrows to a service or Slack thread, and routes through the runtime alert pipeline.

nodes.name: health_command
nodes.summary: Renders local agent + integration health, with optional --watch polling at a configurable rate for live dashboards.

nodes.name: _run_service_investigation
nodes.summary: Helper that builds a synthetic alert for a named service (optionally enriched with a Slack thread) and feeds it into the runtime alert pipeline.

nodes.name: guardrails.py
nodes.summary: Thin Click wrappers for the guardrails subsystem (init, test, audit, list rules) that delegate to app.guardrails.cli.

nodes.name: integrations.py
nodes.summary: `integrations` command group exposing setup/list/show/remove/verify subcommands as thin shims over app.integrations.cli with analytics emission.

nodes.name: onboard.py
nodes.summary: `onboard` command that runs the wizard for first-time setup, plus an `onboard local-llm` variant that configures a local model provider.

nodes.name: remote.py
nodes.summary: Massive remote-agent command surface: configure remote URL/api-key, browse/run investigations against a hosted runtime, manage ops scope (provider/project/service), check health, restart, pull artifacts, and stream LangGraph runs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TYPE_CHECKING-only imports for app.remote.client and app.remote.ops to keep startup fast and avoid circular imports.

nodes.name: _run_remote_interactive
nodes.summary: Interactive remote-agent wizard: probes preflight, surfaces deploy/health choices, lets the user pick which remote to talk to, and dispatches to investigation/ops actions.

nodes.name: remote
nodes.summary: Root `remote` Click group that captures --url and --api-key, persisting them on the click context for all remote subcommands.

nodes.name: remote_ops_status
nodes.summary: `remote ops status` subcommand that reports the live status of the configured ops provider/service, with JSON output support.

nodes.name: remote_ops_logs
nodes.summary: `remote ops logs` subcommand that fetches and tails recent logs from the configured remote ops provider.

nodes.name: remote_ops_restart
nodes.summary: `remote ops restart` subcommand that triggers a service restart through the configured ops provider, gated by --yes confirmation.

nodes.name: remote_health
nodes.summary: `remote health` subcommand that runs run_remote_health_check against the configured base URL and renders or JSON-emits the report.

nodes.name: remote_trigger
nodes.summary: `remote trigger` subcommand that posts a JSON alert payload to the remote runtime, optionally detaching for fire-and-forget mode.

nodes.name: remote_investigate
nodes.summary: `remote investigate` subcommand that streams a remote investigation by default and can fall back to a blocking call or 404-recovery path.

nodes.name: remote_pull
nodes.summary: `remote pull` subcommand that downloads investigation artifacts (latest, all, or a chosen run) into a local output directory.

nodes.name: remote_health.py
nodes.summary: Implementation of the remote health-check report: pretty-prints uptime, integration status, and version drift, and persists the verified base URL when checks succeed.

nodes.name: run_remote_health_check
nodes.summary: Calls the remote agent's /health endpoint via RemoteAgentClient, renders or JSON-emits the report, and persists the base URL on success.

nodes.name: _render_remote_health_report
nodes.summary: Rich-console renderer that formats the remote health report into per-section tables (runtime, integrations, version, build).

nodes.name: tests.py
nodes.summary: `tests` command group: lists synthetic + real RCA test catalog items, dispatches a chosen test, and provides a synthetic-suite runner with optional Grafana mocking.

nodes.name: run_synthetic_suite
nodes.summary: Runs the synthetic RCA suite for a given scenario, building argv flags (mock-grafana, json output) and delegating to the suite runner module.

nodes.name: list_tests
nodes.summary: Lists registered tests from the catalog, optionally filtered by category or substring search.

nodes.name: run_test
nodes.summary: Resolves a test id and either dry-runs (printing the plan) or invokes it against the runtime alert pipeline.

nodes.name: _TestIdType
nodes.summary: Custom Click ParamType that adds shell-completion for test ids by introspecting the test catalog.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Interactive REPL barrel — re-exports `run_repl` from app.cli.interactive_shell.loop (callers use `from app.cli.interactive_shell import run_repl`).

nodes.name: agent_actions.py
nodes.summary: Plans and executes terminal actions from a user prompt: parses slash commands, shell commands, sample-alert and synthetic-test invocations, and LLM-provider switches into a typed PlannedAction sequence.

nodes.name: PlannedAction
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a planned terminal action (kind, payload, position) emitted by the prompt parser and consumed by the executor.

nodes.name: PromptClause
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a parsed clause of a user prompt (text and position), used during multi-clause prompt segmentation.

nodes.name: _plan_clause_actions
nodes.summary: Core planner that classifies a single prompt clause into slash, shell, sample-alert, synthetic-test, or LLM-provider-switch actions, deduping repeated slash commands.

nodes.name: execute_cli_actions
nodes.summary: Executes a planned-action sequence: dispatches slash commands, runs shell/cd commands, fires sample alerts and synthetic tests, and switches LLM providers in order.

nodes.name: plan_cli_actions
nodes.summary: Public wrapper around _plan_actions that returns only the recognized PlannedAction list for a user message.

nodes.name: _run_shell_command
nodes.summary: Runs a shell command via subprocess from the interactive shell, capturing output and rendering it through the themed console.

nodes.name: _run_synthetic_test
nodes.summary: Runs a named synthetic test suite from inside the interactive shell, reusing the synthetic-suite runner used by the tests CLI.

nodes.name: banner.py
nodes.summary: Renders the interactive-shell startup banner with provider/model detection and version info using the shared Rich theme.

nodes.name: render_banner
nodes.summary: Composes and prints the welcome banner with detected LLM provider, current model, and version, falling back gracefully when settings are missing.

nodes.name: detect_provider_model
nodes.summary: Resolves the active LLM provider/model pair from local settings for display in the banner.

nodes.name: cli_agent.py
nodes.summary: LLM-powered CLI assistant: builds a system prompt grounded in the CLI reference and recent history, asks the model for a JSON action plan, parses it, and executes the resulting steps.

nodes.name: answer_cli_agent
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI agent entry: invokes the LLM with a grounded system prompt, parses the returned action plan, and executes each step against the interactive session.

nodes.name: _build_system_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds the grounded system prompt string from CLI reference, optional grounding text, and conversation history for the agent LLM call.

nodes.name: _execute_action_plan
nodes.summary: Executes a parsed JSON action plan in order: shell commands, slash dispatch, follow-up answer text, with safety/print of each step.

nodes.name: cli_help.py
nodes.summary: LLM-grounded help responder for the interactive shell: assembles CLI + docs context and asks the model to answer how-to questions about the tool.

nodes.name: answer_cli_help
nodes.summary: Answers a free-form CLI help question using a grounded prompt over the CLI reference and bundled docs, streaming the response to the themed console.

nodes.name: _build_grounded_prompt
nodes.summary: Composes the system prompt with embedded CLI reference and docs reference for the help-answering LLM call.

nodes.name: cli_reference.py
nodes.summary: Builds the textual CLI reference (commands + slash hints) injected into LLM prompts so the agent answers about real, supported commands.

nodes.name: build_cli_reference_text
nodes.summary: Walks the registered Click command tree and produces a human-readable reference string used as LLM grounding.

nodes.name: commands.py
nodes.summary: Registry and implementations for every interactive-shell slash command (/help, /status, /integrations, /mcp, /model, /health, /doctor, /investigate, /history, /save, /context, /cost, /verbose, /compact, /stop, ...) plus the dispatch_slash entrypoint.

nodes.name: SlashCommand
nodes.summary: Lightweight dataclass describing a slash command (name, handler callable, help text) used by the interactive-shell registry.

nodes.name: dispatch_slash
nodes.summary: Top-level dispatcher that splits a slash-command line into name+args, looks it up in the registry, and invokes the matching handler.

nodes.name: switch_llm_provider
nodes.summary: Switches the active LLM provider (and optionally model), updating local settings and emitting a status line to the console.

nodes.name: _cmd_status
nodes.summary: /status slash command that prints a quick session summary (active provider, integrations count, uptime).

nodes.name: _cmd_integrations
nodes.summary: /integrations slash command that loads verified integrations from the store and renders a Rich table of name/status/details.

nodes.name: _cmd_doctor
nodes.summary: /doctor slash command that runs the doctor checks inside the interactive shell and renders the report.

nodes.name: _cmd_investigate_file
nodes.summary: /investigate slash command that ingests an alert payload from a file path argument and runs it through the runtime alert pipeline.

nodes.name: _cmd_save
nodes.summary: /save slash command that persists the current investigation history (transcript + outputs) to a chosen path.

nodes.name: _cmd_list
nodes.summary: /list slash command that lists templates or registered tests inside the interactive shell.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines the immutable `ReplConfig` dataclass and a three-tier resolution chain (CLI flag → env var → ~/.opensre/config.yml) for the interactive REPL's enabled/layout settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `@dataclass(frozen=True)` with classmethod factories and walrus assignment in elif chains.

nodes.name: ReplConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding `enabled` and `layout` for the interactive shell, with `load()` resolving the three-tier override priority and `from_env()` shorthand.

nodes.name: docs_reference.py
nodes.summary: Discovers and indexes project docs (`docs/*.mdx`), tokenizes/scores user queries against page content, and builds a relevance-ranked context block fed back into the REPL's LLM prompt.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `functools.lru_cache` on `_discover_docs_cached` to memoize filesystem walks; pure-Python BM25-style scoring without external deps.

nodes.name: DocPage
nodes.summary: Lightweight record of a discovered documentation page (slug, relpath, title, body) used for in-memory search.

nodes.name: _discover_docs_cached
nodes.summary: Memoized walk of the docs root that strips frontmatter, extracts titles, and returns a tuple of `DocPage` records suitable for repeated query scoring.

nodes.name: _score
nodes.summary: Scores a `DocPage` against a tokenized query using title/body weighting and frequency, used by `find_relevant_docs` to rank results.

nodes.name: find_relevant_docs
nodes.summary: Returns the top-N most relevant `DocPage` records for a free-text query, applying tokenization and `_score` ranking.

nodes.name: build_docs_index
nodes.summary: Builds a compact textual index of discovered doc titles and slugs to inject into the LLM prompt as a navigation aid.

nodes.name: build_docs_reference_text
nodes.summary: Composes the final docs-reference context block (top results + excerpts, character-budgeted) that the REPL appends to follow-up answers.

nodes.name: follow_up.py
nodes.summary: Implements the REPL's follow-up question handler: summarizes prior evidence and the last investigation state, then asks the LLM client for a contextual answer with a spinner.

nodes.name: answer_follow_up
nodes.summary: Answers a follow-up question by injecting prior session state and evidence summaries into the LLM prompt and streaming the response.

nodes.name: _summarize_evidence
nodes.summary: Compresses accumulated evidence items into a short bullet list suitable for the follow-up LLM prompt budget.

nodes.name: _summarize_last_state
nodes.summary: Renders the last investigation state (alert, hypotheses, conclusion) into a compact prompt-ready string.

nodes.name: history.py
nodes.summary: Tiny module locating and loading the prompt-toolkit history file under `~/.opensre/`, used by the REPL to persist user input across sessions.

nodes.name: loaders.py
nodes.summary: Provides the `llm_loader` context manager that displays a Rich spinner with consistent styling while LLM/agent calls are in flight.

nodes.name: loop.py
nodes.summary: Async REPL main loop wiring prompt-toolkit, slash-command completion, key bindings, and routing between investigation/follow-up/CLI-agent paths — the entry point for the interactive shell.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio with prompt_toolkit's PromptSession; integrates Rich Console for output styling.

nodes.name: SlashCommandCompleter
nodes.summary: Custom prompt-toolkit `Completer` that surfaces slash-command suggestions (with help text) as the user types `/`.

nodes.name: run_repl
nodes.summary: Public entry point that bootstraps the async REPL, handling EOF/KeyboardInterrupt to cleanly exit when the shell terminates.

nodes.name: _repl_main
nodes.summary: Core async loop: reads a prompt, resolves slash commands, dispatches to investigation/follow-up/CLI-agent handlers, and updates session context until the user exits.

nodes.name: _run_one_turn
nodes.summary: Executes a single REPL turn: classifies the input, routes to the matching handler (slash, follow-up, agent, investigation), and records results into the session.

nodes.name: _run_new_alert
nodes.summary: Runs a fresh investigation against the LangGraph pipeline for free-text alerts entered in the REPL, recording state into the session.

nodes.name: _build_prompt_session
nodes.summary: Constructs the prompt-toolkit `PromptSession` with completer, key bindings, history, and styling pre-wired.

nodes.name: _build_prompt_key_bindings
nodes.summary: Returns prompt-toolkit `KeyBindings` for navigating completions and accepting/rejecting suggestions in the REPL.

nodes.name: _build_prompt_style
nodes.summary: Builds the prompt-toolkit `Style` object that colors the prompt prefix and completion menu using OpenSRE's amber/orange palette.

nodes.name: router.py
nodes.summary: Heuristic input classifier: tags REPL input as slash, cli_help, cli_agent, new_alert, or follow_up using keyword/regex/structural cues so the shell can pick the right handler.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python `Literal` types for the InputKind union and frozenset/tuple constants for fast keyword matching.

nodes.name: classify_input
nodes.summary: Top-level classifier that returns the `InputKind` for free-text REPL input by combining length, vocabulary, JSON-shape, and prior-session signals.

nodes.name: _long_line_suggests_incident_narrative
nodes.summary: Returns True when free text contains incident-narrative cues (HTTP codes, timestamps, percentages, on-call vocabulary) suggesting a new alert rather than a question.

nodes.name: _looks_like_json_payload
nodes.summary: Detects whether REPL input is a parseable JSON object/array, which forces classification as a new alert payload.

nodes.name: session.py
nodes.summary: Defines `ReplSession`, the in-memory session state container holding conversation history, last investigation state, accumulated evidence, trust mode, and token usage across REPL turns.

nodes.name: ReplSession
nodes.summary: Mutable session container with `record`, `accumulate_from_state`, and `clear` methods that bridge each REPL turn into the next while merging investigation state into the rolling context.

nodes.name: terminal_intent.py
nodes.summary: Vocabulary-based intent detectors used by the router: alert signals, integration mentions, CLI-agent operational intent, and sample-alert launch shortcuts.

nodes.name: theme.py
nodes.summary: ANSI color constants and the `_hex` converter providing OpenSRE's amber/orange brand palette for the interactive shell.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the investigation entry points (`run_investigation_cli`, streaming variants, session helpers) for the CLI layer.

nodes.name: alert_templates.py
nodes.summary: Builds canned sample alert payloads (Kubernetes, database, latency, etc.) used by the REPL's sample-alert launch flow and onboarding demos.

nodes.name: build_alert_template
nodes.summary: Returns a fully-formed sample alert payload string for the named template (e.g. `kubernetes`, `database`) for use as REPL/demo input.

nodes.name: investigate.py

nodes.languageNotes: Decorator-based tool registration via `@mcp.tool` and Pydantic models for input/output validation.

nodes.name: RunRCAInput
nodes.summary: Pydantic model defining the run_rca tool's input schema: alert payload plus optional name, pipeline, and severity overrides.

nodes.name: RunRCAOutput
nodes.summary: Pydantic model describing the run_rca tool's response with ok flag, optional result dict, and error fields.

nodes.name: run_rca
nodes.summary: MCP-registered tool that validates input, applies label/severity overrides, runs the investigation CLI, and returns a structured result or typed error.

nodes.name: sdk.py
nodes.summary: Programmatic SDK shim that lazily imports `app.pipeline.runners.run_investigation` to keep top-level imports cheap.

nodes.name: graph_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Compatibility shim that re-exports `build_graph` from `app.pipeline.graph` to preserve the older `app.graph_pipeline:build_graph` entrypoint used by deployed LangGraph runtime config.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Public surface of the guardrails package, re-exporting the GuardrailEngine and its singleton accessor for sensitive-content scanning.

nodes.name: audit.py
nodes.summary: Append-only JSONL audit logger that records guardrail rule matches under ~/.opensre/guardrail_audit.jsonl and never raises on write failure.

nodes.name: AuditLogger
nodes.summary: Writes timestamped guardrail-match entries to a JSONL file and reads back the most recent N entries on demand.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Click-based CLI commands (`init`, `test`, `audit`, `rules`) for managing the OpenSRE guardrails configuration and inspecting runtime behaviour.

nodes.name: cmd_init
nodes.summary: Bootstraps a starter guardrails YAML config in the user's home directory if one is not already present.

nodes.name: cmd_test
nodes.summary: Runs the guardrail engine against a user-supplied text snippet and reports matches, redactions, and blocking decisions.

nodes.name: cmd_audit
nodes.summary: Prints the most recent guardrail audit log entries from the JSONL file as formatted JSON.

nodes.name: cmd_rules
nodes.summary: Lists all configured guardrail rules with their patterns, keywords, action, and enabled state.

nodes.name: engine.py
nodes.summary: Core guardrail engine that scans text against compiled rules, merges overlapping match spans, applies redact/block/audit actions, and exposes a process-wide singleton.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses NamedTuple `_MergedSpan` plus longest-keyword-wins overlap merging to produce stable redactions across overlapping rules.

nodes.name: ScanMatch
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing a single rule match with its rule name, action, matched text, and span offsets.

nodes.name: ScanResult
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass aggregating all matches found in a scan plus blocked flag and the rule names that triggered blocking.

nodes.name: GuardrailBlockedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when input text matches one or more blocking guardrail rules.

nodes.name: GuardrailEngine
nodes.summary: Scans text against configured rules, merges overlapping spans, redacts or blocks based on actions, and writes audit entries via the injected AuditLogger.

nodes.name: rules.py
nodes.summary: Guardrail rule dataclass, action enum, and YAML loader that compiles patterns and keywords from the user's `~/.opensre/guardrails.yml` configuration.

nodes.name: GuardrailAction
nodes.summary: Enum naming the three guardrail actions: REDACT, BLOCK, and AUDIT.

nodes.name: GuardrailRule
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding a guardrail rule's name, action, compiled regex patterns, keywords, replacement string, and enabled flag.

nodes.name: load_rules
nodes.summary: Loads guardrail rules from a YAML file, returning an empty list when the file is missing or malformed.

nodes.name: _parse_rule
nodes.summary: Parses a single rule dict from YAML, compiling regexes and validating that at least one pattern or keyword is present.

nodes.name: incident_window.py
nodes.summary: Provides the IncidentWindow value object and resolver that derives a single half-open `[since, until)` UTC window from raw alerts (Alertmanager, Datadog, PagerDuty, CloudWatch) so all time-aware tools share a consistent investigation range.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclass with `__post_init__` invariants and per-format anchor-extraction strategies; rejects naive datetimes.

nodes.name: IncidentWindow
nodes.summary: Frozen, validated `[since, until)` UTC window produced by the resolver and consumed by every time-aware investigation tool.

nodes.name: _alertmanager_anchor
nodes.summary: Extracts the firing-time anchor from an Alertmanager payload, preferring `startsAt` and falling back across labels and annotations.

nodes.name: _datadog_anchor
nodes.summary: Extracts the firing-time anchor from a Datadog monitor payload via firedAt, transition timestamps, or alert text fields.

nodes.name: _pagerduty_anchor
nodes.summary: Extracts the firing-time anchor from a PagerDuty incident payload, preferring `created_at` then incident change timestamps.

nodes.name: _cloudwatch_anchor
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a CloudWatch alarm payload to extract the StateChangeTime or NewStateReason timestamp.

nodes.name: _extract_anchor
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the per-format anchor extractor based on payload shape, returning the first plausible firing timestamp.

nodes.name: resolve_incident_window
nodes.summary: Resolves the canonical IncidentWindow for an alert, honouring caller overrides, lookback caps, and forward-buffer minutes against a wall-clock anchor.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the integrations layer that hosts config models, store, verification, and per-vendor builders.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Module entrypoint (`python -m app.integrations`) that dispatches setup/list/show/remove/verify subcommands for managing integration credentials.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses sys.argv, prints help when requested, and dispatches integration-management subcommands to the underlying CLI handlers.

nodes.name: _catalog_impl.py
nodes.summary: Internal implementation of the integration catalog that classifies integrations by service, parses per-instance env vars across ~30 vendors, and resolves the effective merged config consumed by the runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: Single ~1700-line module owning the multi-vendor classification, env merging, and effective-config publishing pipeline.

nodes.name: classify_integrations
nodes.summary: Classifies a flat integrations dict into per-service buckets and instance siblings, applying skip and direct-publish registry rules.

nodes.name: _classify_service_instance
nodes.summary: Massive per-service classifier that normalizes credentials and config for nearly every supported integration into a uniform classified record.

nodes.name: _parse_instances_env
nodes.summary: Parses environment variables encoding multi-instance integration configs (e.g. additional Datadog or Sentry workspaces) into a list of per-instance records.

nodes.name: load_env_integrations
nodes.summary: Reads OS environment variables for every supported integration vendor and constructs the env-sourced integration dict consumed by the catalog merger.

nodes.name: merge_local_integrations
nodes.summary: Merges local store integrations with env-sourced integrations, with the local store taking precedence per service.

nodes.name: resolve_effective_integrations
nodes.summary: Produces the EffectiveIntegrations object the runtime consumes by classifying, merging, and publishing per-service entries with provenance metadata.

nodes.name: _relational.py
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for relational DB integrations: env-var coercion, RelationalConfigBase Pydantic field validators, and a stored-or-env config resolver.

nodes.name: RelationalConfigBase
nodes.summary: Pydantic base model providing reusable host/database/username field validators for relational DB integration configs.

nodes.name: resolve_stored_or_env_config
nodes.summary: Generic resolver that builds a relational integration config from the local store first, then env, then bare identifiers — typed via PEP 695 generics.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PEP 695 generic syntax `def resolve_stored_or_env_config[ConfigT](...)` for inline type parameters.

nodes.name: _validators.py
nodes.summary: Reusable field-validator factories (normalize_str, normalize_url, normalize_bearer, normalize_bool_str) that replace dozens of one-line validators across the integration config models.

nodes.name: _verification_adapters.py
nodes.summary: Service-specific verifiers and shared adapter factories used by `opensre verify` to probe integration credentials for Grafana, AWS, Slack, Datadog, Tracer, and many more vendors.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes `build_validation_verifier`/`build_probe_verifier` higher-order factories with per-vendor `_verify_*` functions to keep a uniform `(source, config) -> result-dict` signature.

nodes.name: build_validation_verifier
nodes.summary: Higher-order factory that builds a verifier function from a config builder and validator pair, returning a uniform verify result dict.

nodes.name: build_probe_verifier
nodes.summary: Higher-order factory that builds a verifier which constructs a vendor client and probes connectivity, returning a uniform verify result dict.

nodes.name: _build_sts_client
nodes.summary: Builds a boto3 STS client supporting role assumption and external IDs from an AWSIntegrationConfig.

nodes.name: _verify_grafana
nodes.summary: Verifies Grafana credentials by probing each configured Loki/Tempo/Prometheus datasource and surfacing per-datasource status.

nodes.name: _verify_aws
nodes.summary: Verifies AWS credentials by calling STS GetCallerIdentity through the locally built boto3 client.

nodes.name: _verify_slack
nodes.summary: Verifies a Slack webhook by validating its config and optionally posting a real test message when send_slack_test is enabled.

nodes.name: _verify_tracer
nodes.summary: Verifies the Tracer integration credentials by extracting org id from the JWT and probing the Tracer API base URL.

nodes.name: airflow.py

nodes.name: _request_json

nodes.name: validate_airflow_config

nodes.name: get_recent_airflow_failures

nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately placed at app/ root to side-step a pipeline -> nodes circular import.

nodes.name: llm_credentials.py
nodes.summary: Resolves and persists LLM API keys via OS keyring with environment-variable fallback, supporting an OPENSRE_DISABLE_KEYRING escape hatch for headless or restricted environments.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps the cross-platform `keyring` library with defensive try/except so missing backends never crash the agent.

nodes.name: resolve_llm_api_key
nodes.summary: Returns the API key for a given env var, preferring the live process env then falling back to the OS keyring entry when keyring isn't disabled.

nodes.name: save_llm_api_key
nodes.summary: Persists an API key into the OS keyring under the OpenSRE service namespace, swallowing keyring backend errors so onboarding never hard-fails.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint that parses args, builds an alert payload (or loads one from disk), and dispatches to the synchronous run-investigation flow.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI driver: chooses between alert-template generation and full investigation execution based on parsed argv.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Public surface of the masking package — re-exports MaskingContext, MaskingPolicy, find_identifiers and the kind/identifier types so callers depend on `app.masking` rather than internal modules.

nodes.name: context.py
nodes.summary: Stateful MaskingContext that detects sensitive identifiers in outbound text, swaps them for stable placeholders (e.g. `<POD_0>`), and reverses the substitution on the response, with serialisation hooks so the map survives across LangGraph turns.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses placeholder counters keyed by IdentifierKind so the same value always maps to the same token within a session.

nodes.name: MaskingContext
nodes.summary: Stateful masking session: `mask`/`unmask` operate on a single string; `mask_value`/`unmask_value` recursively walk Mapping/Sequence containers. Holds the per-run identifier→token map and the active MaskingPolicy.

nodes.name: detectors.py
nodes.summary: Regex-based detectors for sensitive infrastructure identifiers (pods, clusters, hosts, IPs, emails, account IDs) plus overlap resolution to pick the longest, most-specific match per span.

nodes.name: find_identifiers
nodes.summary: Scans `text` against built-in detectors plus operator-supplied extras, filtered by the active MaskingPolicy, and returns non-overlapping DetectedIdentifier matches.

nodes.name: _resolve_overlaps
nodes.summary: Sorts overlapping regex matches and keeps only the longest non-overlapping ones, ensuring nested patterns (e.g. IP inside a hostname) collapse to a single masking decision.

nodes.name: DetectedIdentifier
nodes.summary: Lightweight dataclass-style record describing a detected identifier (kind, value, span) used as the unit of work between detection and masking.

nodes.name: policy.py
nodes.summary: Defines the MaskingPolicy (which identifier kinds are enabled, plus operator-supplied extra regex patterns) loaded from environment variables, with strict validation against ALL_KINDS.

nodes.name: MaskingPolicy
nodes.summary: Pydantic-style policy model: coerces enabled-kinds inputs, validates extra regex patterns, exposes from_env loader and is_kind_enabled lookup.

nodes.name: compile_extra_patterns
nodes.summary: Compiles operator-supplied extra-pattern strings into runtime regex objects, skipping malformed patterns with a warning so a single bad rule never disables masking.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the LangGraph node entry points (adapt_window, extract_alert, plan_actions, publish_findings, resolve_integrations, root_cause_diagnosis) for graph assembly.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Adaptive-window package barrel — exposes node_adapt_window (LangGraph entry) and adapt_incident_window (pure rule) so the rule logic stays unit-testable.

nodes.name: node.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that runs the incident-window adaptation rule, emits debug output on changes, and returns a state delta with the widened window plus an audit entry.

nodes.name: node_adapt_window
nodes.summary: Invokes adapt_incident_window on the current state and converts the rule's plain-dict output into a LangGraph state delta, logging when the window is actually widened.

nodes.name: rules.py
nodes.summary: Pure decision logic for incident-window expansion: detects empty deploy-timeline iterations and proposes a strictly-wider window, with an injected now_fn for deterministic tests.

nodes.name: adapt_incident_window
nodes.summary: Inspects the previous iteration's executed actions and, when deploy-timeline came back empty, returns a strictly-wider incident window plus an audit-trail entry; otherwise returns no change.

nodes.name: _last_iteration_actions
nodes.summary: Pulls the executed-actions dict from the most recent investigation iteration, defending against shape drift in upstream state.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Tiny LangGraph node that lifts auth metadata (user_id, token, etc.) from the runtime config into the agent state so downstream nodes can read it uniformly.

nodes.name: chat.py
nodes.summary: Conversational chat surface: builds a tools-bound chat LLM (per provider), routes between general and tool-using agents, applies guardrails to outgoing messages, and executes returned tool calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Provider-specific model resolution lives in `_resolve_models` so adding a new vendor only touches one switch.

nodes.name: router_node
nodes.summary: Decides whether the current chat turn should be handled by the tool-using agent or the general-conversation node based on the inbound message and state.

nodes.name: chat_agent_node
nodes.summary: Runs the tool-bound chat LLM against the conversation history, applies outbound guardrails, and emits the assistant message (with any tool_calls) into state.

nodes.name: general_node
nodes.summary: Plain-chat fallback that calls the chat LLM without tools when the router decides the user only needs a conversational reply.

nodes.name: tool_executor_node
nodes.summary: Walks the assistant's tool_calls, looks each up in the registered-tool registry, executes them, and appends ToolMessages back into the conversation.

nodes.name: _resolve_models
nodes.summary: Picks the right primary chat model and tool model for the configured provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), raising UnsupportedChatProviderError on unknown vendors.

nodes.name: _build_chat_model
nodes.summary: Constructs the LangChain chat-model client for a given provider/model name, threading API keys and provider-specific options.

nodes.name: _get_chat_llm
nodes.summary: High-level helper that returns either a tools-bound or plain chat LLM by composing _resolve_models, _build_chat_model and bind_tools.

nodes.name: _apply_guardrails_to_messages
nodes.summary: Runs each outbound assistant message through the guardrail engine and rewrites or blocks content per policy before it reaches the user.

nodes.name: _normalize_messages
nodes.summary: Converts heterogeneous message inputs (LangChain message objects vs plain dicts) into the role/content shape expected by the chat LLM.

nodes.name: UnsupportedChatProviderError
nodes.summary: Raised when the configured chat provider does not have a model mapping in _resolve_models.

nodes.summary: Merges fields from extracted AlertDetails back into the raw alert dict so downstream nodes see a unified payload regardless of source.

nodes.name: _make_problem_md
nodes.summary: Renders a short markdown problem statement from AlertDetails fields, used as the human-readable lede in Slack and final reports.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models defining the structured-output contract for alert extraction: AlertExtractionInput (LLM input) and AlertDetails (typed parsed output).

nodes.name: AlertDetails
nodes.summary: Canonical AlertDetails schema (title, severity, summary, service, environment, etc.) used as the structured output target for the extraction LLM and as input to downstream investigation nodes.

nodes.name: AlertExtractionInput
nodes.summary: Wrapper input model for alert-extraction LLM calls, carrying the raw alert text and source metadata.

nodes.name: execute_actions

nodes.name: ActionExecutionResult

nodes.name: _load_opensre_telemetry_into_evidence

nodes.name: post_process.py
nodes.summary: Post-processes investigation tool execution results into a normalized evidence summary covering AWS, Datadog, Grafana, GitHub, EKS, Argo CD, and other source-specific payloads. Tracks executed hypotheses, classifies failed actions, and produces aggregated evidence consumed by the planning loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of dispatch dictionaries mapping tool names to per-source mapper functions, enabling extensible tool result handling without conditional sprawl.

nodes.name: merge_evidence
nodes.summary: Merges newly observed execution results into the running evidence dictionary, deduplicating entries and preserving ordering for downstream summarization.

nodes.name: track_hypothesis
nodes.summary: Records an executed investigation hypothesis with rationale, plan audit, and failure metadata into the per-loop hypothesis history list.

nodes.name: build_evidence_summary

nodes.name: summarize_execution_results
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestration entry that merges new execution results, builds the evidence summary, tracks hypotheses, and returns the consolidated investigation state delta.

nodes.name: _build_failed_action_records

nodes.name: FailedAction

nodes.name: ExecutedHypothesis

nodes.name: build_prompt.py

nodes.summary: Produces a richly formatted hint block describing every available investigation source (logs, metrics, traces, repos, deployments) so the planner LLM can reason over capabilities.

nodes.summary: Assembles the full investigation planning prompt combining problem markdown, executed hypotheses, available actions, sources, and memory context for the planner LLM.

nodes.name: plan_actions_with_llm
nodes.summary: Invokes the planner LLM with the assembled prompt and parses its structured response into a typed plan model.

nodes.summary: Filters and ranks candidate actions against available sources, executed hypotheses, and tool budget to produce the final ordered action list.

nodes.name: apply_tool_budget
nodes.summary: Trims the planned action list to fit within the configured tool execution budget for a single investigation loop.

nodes.name: get_blocked_action_names
nodes.summary: Derives the set of action names already executed and therefore blocked from re-running in the next planning iteration.

nodes.name: detect_sources.py
nodes.summary: Inspects raw alert payloads, alert context, and resolved integrations to detect every investigation source available for the current alert (AWS, Grafana, Datadog, GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Splunk, Coralogix, Vercel, EKS, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: Single ~1280-line orchestrator function that incrementally probes raw_alert/context/resolved_integrations to populate an AvailableSources contract; relies heavily on type guards and URL parsing.

nodes.name: detect_sources
nodes.summary: Walks a raw alert payload, alert context, and resolved integrations to determine every investigation source available, returning a normalized AvailableSources structure for the planner.

nodes.name: _alert_time_range_minutes
nodes.summary: Computes a sensible historical lookback window in minutes from alert metadata, defaulting based on alert type when explicit timestamps are absent.

nodes.name: _alert_since_iso
nodes.summary: Derives an ISO-8601 'since' timestamp for log/metric queries from the alert's effective time range.

nodes.name: _parse_repo_url
nodes.summary: Parses a generic repository URL into owner/repo components, falling back gracefully on malformed or partial inputs.

nodes.name: _parse_bitbucket_repo_url
nodes.summary: Parses a Bitbucket Cloud or Server repository URL into workspace, repo slug, and optional path components.

nodes.name: extract_keywords.py
nodes.summary: Extracts deduplicated keywords from problem markdown and alert names to seed downstream search queries (logs, traces, code search).

nodes.name: extract_keywords
nodes.summary: Tokenizes problem markdown and alert name, filters stop words, and returns ordered unique keywords for query construction.

nodes.name: node.py

nodes.name: node_plan_actions

nodes.name: InvestigationPlan

nodes.name: plan_actions.py

nodes.name: plan_actions
nodes.summary: Orchestrates one planning cycle: detects sources, seeds candidate actions, calls the LLM planner, and assembles the final plan with retrieval intent and audit metadata.

nodes.name: detect_reroute_trigger
nodes.summary: Inspects current evidence, available sources, and executed hypotheses to determine when the investigation should reroute to a different source category.

nodes.name: _ensure_seed_actions_available

nodes.name: _seed_plan_actions
