nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines ToolMetadata (a strict pydantic schema for tool declarations) and BaseTool (the abstract base every class-based investigation tool inherits from).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pydantic StrictConfigModel and ABC to enforce metadata declared as ClassVars on subclasses, with validation in __init_subclass__.

nodes.name: ToolMetadata
nodes.summary: Strict pydantic schema describing the metadata every BaseTool subclass must declare, including name, description, input schema, evidence source, and retrieval controls.

nodes.name: BaseTool
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for class-based tools; declares metadata as ClassVars, validates them via ToolMetadata in __init_subclass__, and exposes is_available/extract_params/run hooks.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package facade that re-exports the investigation action registry API: action listing, prioritization helpers, and the InvestigationAction alias.

nodes.name: actions.py
nodes.summary: Thin adapter that exposes get_available_actions(), returning the investigation-surface RegisteredTool snapshot from the canonical tool registry.

nodes.name: docstring_parser.py
nodes.summary: Parses Google-style docstrings to extract use cases, input parameters, output keys, and the leading description block for tool registration metadata.

nodes.name: extract_inputs
nodes.summary: Parses Args/Inputs sections of a docstring and reconciles them against the function signature to produce input parameter metadata.

nodes.name: extract_outputs
nodes.summary: Parses the Returns/Outputs section of a docstring into a dict mapping output keys to their descriptions for tool metadata.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Compatibility shim that aliases the legacy InvestigationAction name to the canonical RegisteredTool dataclass.

nodes.name: prioritization.py
nodes.summary: Ranks the available investigation actions for a given alert by scoring each tool's source/use-cases against alert keywords and integration availability.

nodes.name: get_prioritized_actions_with_reasons
nodes.summary: Scores each available action against alert keywords and configured sources, returning the ranked actions with human-readable reasons.

nodes.name: registered_tool.py
nodes.summary: Defines RegisteredTool, the canonical runtime tool dataclass shared by class-based and function-based tools, with factories from BaseTool subclasses or decorated functions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass + typing.get_type_hints to infer JSON Schemas from Python annotations, supporting Optional unwrapping and Literal/list/dict mapping.

nodes.name: _normalize_surfaces
nodes.summary: Validates and de-duplicates a tool's surface tuple (e.g. investigation, chat), raising on unknown surfaces.

nodes.name: _annotation_to_json_schema
nodes.summary: Maps a Python type annotation (str, int, list, dict, Literal, Optional) to a JSON Schema fragment used for tool input schemas.

nodes.name: infer_input_schema
nodes.summary: Inspects a function's signature and type hints to synthesize a JSON Schema describing required and optional parameters.

nodes.name: RegisteredTool
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass that holds the runtime metadata, callable, surfaces, and factories for both BaseTool subclasses and decorated function tools.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Canonical tool registry that walks the app.tools package via pkgutil, collects RegisteredTool instances, and exposes cached lookups by surface (investigation/chat).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pkgutil.iter_modules for runtime discovery and functools.lru_cache to memoize the registry snapshot per surface.

nodes.name: _collect_registered_tools_from_module
nodes.summary: Inspects an imported tool module for BaseTool subclasses or decorated functions and yields their RegisteredTool representations.

nodes.name: _load_registry_snapshot
nodes.summary: Iterates discoverable tool modules, imports each, and assembles the deduplicated tuple of RegisteredTool entries that backs the registry cache.

nodes.name: get_registered_tools
nodes.summary: Public API returning all RegisteredTool entries filtered to a given surface (investigation or chat).

nodes.name: get_registered_tool_map
nodes.summary: Public API returning a name-keyed dict of RegisteredTool entries for the requested surface.

nodes.name: resolve_tool_display_name
nodes.summary: Looks up the human-readable display name for a registered tool by its canonical name, falling back to the canonical name if no override exists.

nodes.name: run_diagnostic_code.py
nodes.summary: Function tool that runs short ad-hoc Python diagnostic code inside the sandboxed runner with a bounded timeout, returning stdout/stderr/result.

nodes.name: run_diagnostic_code
nodes.summary: Tool action that invokes the Python sandbox runner with provided code and inputs, clamping the requested timeout for safety.

nodes.name: tool_decorator.py
nodes.summary: Provides the @tool decorator with overloaded signatures that registers function-based tools or annotates existing BaseTool subclasses with surface and metadata info.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses typing.overload for IDE-friendly signatures and stores RegisteredTool metadata on the wrapped target via a sentinel attribute.

nodes.name: tool
nodes.summary: Implementation of the @tool decorator that detects whether to attach RegisteredTool metadata to a BaseTool or wrap a plain function based on supplied options.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports tool utilities: code-host helpers, evidence compaction, log deduplication, data validation, and database warnings.

nodes.name: availability.py
nodes.summary: Helpers that check whether EKS, Datadog, or CloudWatch integrations are configured for a given sources mapping, used by tool is_available hooks.

nodes.name: code_host_unavailable.py
nodes.summary: Returns a standard placeholder payload signaling that a code-host integration (GitHub, GitLab, etc.) is not configured for the current investigation.

nodes.name: compaction.py
nodes.summary: Evidence-shrinking helpers that bound list lengths, truncate long messages, and produce summary counts so logs/traces/metrics fit in the LLM context window.

nodes.name: compact_logs
nodes.summary: Caps log lists by count and per-entry character length so log payloads stay within the LLM context budget.

nodes.name: compact_traces
nodes.summary: Limits the number of traces and spans-per-trace returned by tracing tools to keep payloads compact.

nodes.name: compact_metrics
nodes.summary: Trims metric series by total count and datapoints-per-series so metric tool outputs remain bounded.

nodes.name: compact_invocations
nodes.summary: Caps invocation lists and per-invocation embedded log counts so AWS Lambda-style invocation payloads stay bounded.

nodes.name: data_validation.py
nodes.summary: Host-metrics validator that flags impossible or suspicious values (e.g. memory percentages over 100, units mismatches) and emits structured ValidationIssue records.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a pydantic-style ValidationIssue dataclass plus a stateful MetricsValidator class that mutates a shared issues list across heuristic checks.

nodes.name: ValidationIssue
nodes.summary: Immutable record describing a single validation issue: field, raw value, type, severity, explanation, and suggested fix.

nodes.name: MetricsValidator
nodes.summary: Heuristic validator for host metrics that checks memory, CPU, disk, and flat metrics for impossible percentages, unit mismatches, and inconsistencies, returning ValidationIssue records.

nodes.name: validate_host_metrics
nodes.summary: Public entry point that runs MetricsValidator over a metrics payload and returns the metrics enriched with any validation issues detected.

nodes.name: db_warnings.py
nodes.summary: Provides default_db_warning, a tiny helper that returns a standard cautionary message recommending verification before acting on database evidence.

nodes.name: eks_workload_helper.py
nodes.summary: Shared EKS helper utilities that extract AWS credentials, workload identifiers (cluster/namespace/workload), and cluster parameters from heterogeneous source dicts feeding the EKS tools.

nodes.name: extract_workload_params
nodes.summary: Extracts cluster_name, namespace, and workload_name from a list of source dicts, normalizing keys for downstream EKS tool calls.

nodes.name: extract_cluster_params
nodes.summary: Extracts cluster_name and region from heterogeneous source dicts so EKS cluster-level tools can run with consistent parameters.

nodes.name: log_compaction.py
nodes.summary: Log post-processing utilities that deduplicate noisy log lines, classify error types, and build a compact taxonomy with sample messages so the LLM-facing tool outputs stay within token budgets.
nodes.languageNotes: Heuristic message normalization strips IDs, timestamps, IPs, and UUIDs to collapse near-duplicate log lines into a single bucket.

nodes.name: deduplicate_logs
nodes.summary: Collapses near-duplicate log entries by normalized message into representative samples, keeping count/timespan metadata while honoring an output cap.

nodes.name: build_error_taxonomy
nodes.summary: Groups logs into an error-type taxonomy with extracted components and representative sample messages to give the LLM a structured view of failure modes.

nodes.name: compact_logs
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs deduplication and taxonomy generation to produce a compact, LLM-friendly representation of a log batch.

nodes.name: _extract_components
nodes.summary: Heuristically extracts service/component identifiers from log messages to enrich taxonomy buckets with origin information.

nodes.name: sql_wrapper.py
nodes.summary: Wrapper that resolves the effective database name for SQL tool calls and surfaces a default-database warning so users know when a fallback was used.

nodes.name: call_db_tool_with_default_db_warning
nodes.summary: Resolves a database name via the supplied resolver, invokes the database caller, and appends a warning when the default database had to be used.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports the shared domain types (EvidenceSource, RetrievalIntent/Controls, ToolSurface) decoupled from any single feature module.

nodes.name: evidence.py
nodes.summary: Defines EvidenceSource, the canonical Literal enumeration of every supported observability/data source identifier (Grafana, Datadog, EKS, Sentry, GitHub, databases, etc.) used across tools and state.

nodes.name: retrieval.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models describing structured retrieval intents (time bounds, filters, limits, field selection, aggregation) and per-tool support metadata used by the planner to translate LLM-issued queries into concrete tool invocations.

nodes.name: TimeBounds
nodes.summary: Pydantic model expressing a query time window via absolute start/end timestamps or a relative lookback in minutes.

nodes.name: FilterCondition
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a single field/operator/value filter applied to a retrieval query.

nodes.name: FieldSelection
nodes.summary: Pydantic model describing which fields to include or exclude from a retrieval result set.

nodes.name: AggregationSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for aggregation queries (group_by, function, optional field, time bucket) with a validator enforcing that non-count aggregations specify a field.

nodes.name: RetrievalIntent
nodes.summary: Composite Pydantic model bundling time bounds, filters, limits, field selection, and aggregation into a single retrieval plan with a has_controls helper.

nodes.name: RetrievalControls
nodes.summary: Per-tool capability metadata describing which retrieval controls (time, filters, limits, fields, aggregation) a given tool actually supports, with helpers to query support flags.

nodes.name: tools.py
nodes.summary: Defines ToolSurface, the Literal type enumerating where a tool may appear (investigation pipeline, chat surface, etc.).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the cross-cutting app.utils module — utility submodules are imported directly by their callers.

nodes.name: cfg_helpers.py
nodes.summary: CfgHelpers static class with environment-variable lookups and LLM-provider resolution helpers used by the configuration layer.

nodes.name: CfgHelpers
nodes.summary: Static helper namespace bundling first_env_or_default, resolve_llm_provider, and get_clean_env_value for ergonomic config resolution.

nodes.name: coercion.py
nodes.summary: Tiny coercion helpers; currently exposes safe_int that returns a default when integer parsing fails.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Environment-driven configuration helpers covering Grafana Cloud account credentials, OTLP/OTel endpoints and protocols, hosted log/metric/trace IDs, and resource attribute construction.
nodes.languageNotes: Module is organized as 30+ small accessor functions over os.environ rather than a Pydantic settings class, keeping the env-var contract explicit and testable.

nodes.name: load_env
nodes.summary: Loads a .env file into the process environment with optional override of existing variables.

nodes.name: configure_grafana_cloud
nodes.summary: Writes Grafana Cloud OTLP credentials and endpoint configuration into the supplied env file so subsequent processes can authenticate.

nodes.name: apply_otel_env_defaults
nodes.summary: Applies sensible OpenTelemetry exporter defaults (protocol, endpoint, headers) to the environment when not already set.

nodes.name: validate_grafana_cloud_config
nodes.summary: Validates that the Grafana Cloud OTLP environment is internally consistent (matching protocol, endpoint, and credentials).

nodes.name: get_account_datasource_uids
nodes.summary: Returns the list of Grafana datasource UIDs configured for a given account ID via environment variables.

nodes.name: list_account_ids
nodes.summary: Discovers all Grafana account IDs declared in the environment by scanning known prefixed env-var patterns.

nodes.name: delivery_transport.py
nodes.summary: Shared HTTP transport for outbound delivery integrations (Slack, Discord, Telegram, ingest); wraps httpx.post into a uniform DeliveryResponse with structured error handling.

nodes.name: DeliveryResponse
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing the result of an outbound HTTP delivery call: ok flag, status code, parsed JSON, raw text, and error metadata.

nodes.name: post_json
nodes.summary: Posts a JSON payload via httpx with redirect handling, parses the response, and returns a DeliveryResponse with normalized error info.

nodes.name: discord_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Discord delivery utilities that authenticate as a bot, post messages with embeds, create threads, and emit redacted error info — used to publish RCA reports to Discord channels.

nodes.name: post_discord_message
nodes.summary: Posts a message with optional embeds to a Discord channel using the Discord bot REST API and returns the parsed delivery response.

nodes.name: create_discord_thread
nodes.summary: Creates a Discord thread anchored to an existing channel message so follow-up RCA discussion has a dedicated thread.

nodes.name: send_discord_report
nodes.summary: High-level helper that composes a Discord embed payload from an RCA report and sends it via post_discord_message.

nodes.name: ingest_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Ingest API delivery layer that builds investigation payloads from agent state, posts them to the hosted ingest endpoint, derives investigation URLs, and threads the URL back into Slack messages.

nodes.name: build_ingest_payload
nodes.summary: Assembles the ingest API payload from agent state (severity, source, thread, summary fields) for the hosted investigation endpoint.

nodes.name: get_investigation_url
nodes.summary: Builds the public investigation URL for a given org slug and investigation id so users can deep-link from notifications.

nodes.name: send_ingest
nodes.summary: Posts the constructed ingest payload to the hosted investigation API and surfaces the response or structured error to callers.

nodes.name: create_investigation_and_attach_url
nodes.summary: Creates a hosted investigation via the ingest API and patches the resulting URL into a Slack message and summary so users can jump straight to the investigation view.

nodes.name: sentry_sdk.py
nodes.summary: Sentry SDK initialization — reads SENTRY_DSN and SENTRY_TRACES_SAMPLE_RATE directly from os.getenv (not from app.config) so the OTLP exporter can be initialized before pydantic settings load.

nodes.name: init_sentry
nodes.summary: Initializes the Sentry SDK using configured DSN, environment, release tag, and trace sample rate from the app's settings.

nodes.name: slack_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Slack delivery layer covering reaction add/remove/swap, action-button block construction, and posting reports via three transport modes — direct bot token, hosted webapp relay, and incoming webhook.
nodes.languageNotes: Three coexistent posting paths (_post_direct, _post_via_webapp, _post_via_incoming_webhook) let the same report code work in self-hosted, hosted, and webhook-only deployments.

nodes.name: add_reaction
nodes.summary: Adds an emoji reaction to a Slack message via reactions.add, used for status-change feedback (e.g., investigating, resolved).

nodes.name: remove_reaction
nodes.summary: Removes an emoji reaction from a Slack message via reactions.remove.

nodes.name: swap_reaction
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces one Slack reaction with another to signal a status transition on the source message.

nodes.name: build_action_blocks
nodes.summary: Builds the Slack Block Kit action blocks (View Investigation button, etc.) attached to RCA report messages.

nodes.name: send_slack_report
nodes.summary: Top-level Slack report sender that picks the right transport (direct, webapp, or incoming webhook) based on configured credentials and posts the report blocks.

nodes.name: _post_direct
nodes.summary: Posts a Slack message using a direct bot token via chat.postMessage with redacted error reporting.

nodes.name: _post_via_webapp
nodes.summary: Posts a Slack message by relaying through the hosted webapp Slack proxy when no direct bot token is configured locally.

nodes.name: _post_via_incoming_webhook
nodes.summary: Posts a Slack message via an incoming webhook URL when neither bot token nor webapp relay is available.

nodes.name: telegram_delivery.py
nodes.summary: Telegram bot delivery utilities that post messages with reply threading, redact bot tokens from logs, and install a logging filter to keep tokens out of httpx logs.

nodes.name: post_telegram_message
nodes.summary: Posts a message to a Telegram chat via the Bot API with optional reply threading and parse mode, redacting tokens from any error output.

nodes.name: send_telegram_report
nodes.summary: High-level helper that formats an RCA report as Telegram-flavored text and sends it via post_telegram_message.

nodes.name: truncation.py
nodes.summary: Tiny string-truncation helper used by delivery formatters to keep payloads under integration-specific length limits.

nodes.name: url_validation.py
nodes.summary: URL validation helpers that allow only HTTPS, or HTTP for loopback hosts, used to harden user-supplied integration endpoints.

nodes.name: validate_https_or_loopback_http_url
nodes.summary: Raises a descriptive validation error when a service URL is neither HTTPS nor an HTTP loopback address, used by integration config validators.

nodes.name: version.py
nodes.summary: Exposes get_version which reads the installed package version (or a fallback) so the CLI, web health endpoint, and Sentry release tagging share a single source of truth.

nodes.name: get_version
nodes.summary: Returns the installed opensre package version via importlib.metadata, falling back to a default string when not packaged.

nodes.name: webapp.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI web application exposing a /health endpoint that reports graph-load status, LLM configuration validity, version, and environment for liveness checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Returns 200 only when both the LangGraph pipeline module is importable and LLMSettings.from_env validates; otherwise returns 503.

nodes.name: HealthResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model for /health describing ok flag, version, graph_loaded, llm_configured, and environment fields.

nodes.name: get_health_response
nodes.summary: Computes and returns the HealthResponse by inspecting whether the graph pipeline is loaded and LLM settings validate.

nodes.name: health
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler for GET /health returning HealthResponse and setting status 200 or 503 based on overall readiness.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file enumerating every supported configuration variable — LLM provider keys, observability backends, integration credentials, runtime flags — so contributors can copy it to .env and fill in real values.
nodes.languageNotes: Single source of truth for the env-var contract that app/utils/config.py and app/config.py both consume.

nodes.name: bug_report.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue form template for bug reports collecting reproduction steps, environment, and severity from contributors.

nodes.name: config.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue chooser configuration that disables blank issues and provides links to docs and discussion channels.

nodes.name: feature_request.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue form template for feature requests capturing motivation, proposed behavior, and use case from contributors.

nodes.name: improvement.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template scaffolding the structured form for filing improvement requests against the opensre repository.

nodes.name: PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
nodes.summary: Pull request template prompting contributors for description, testing notes, and the four checklist items required before review.

nodes.name: codeql-config.yml
nodes.summary: CodeQL scanner configuration restricting static-analysis paths/queries used by the GitHub security workflow.

nodes.name: dependabot.yml
nodes.summary: Dependabot configuration enabling automated dependency update PRs for the project's package ecosystems.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore manifest excluding generated artifacts, fixtures, and large binaries from the understand-anything knowledge graph scan.

nodes.name: domain-graph.json
nodes.summary: Persisted business-domain knowledge graph derived by the understand-anything plugin, capturing top-level domains and flow relationships.

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Per-file hash fingerprints used by the understand-anything plugin for incremental graph re-analysis cache invalidation.

nodes.name: knowledge-graph.json
nodes.summary: Materialized full knowledge graph (~50k lines) with all nodes and edges produced by the understand-anything pipeline for downstream tools.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Lightweight metadata sidecar describing the most recent understand-anything analysis run (version, timestamp, language counts).

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Tracer development reference for AI coding agents, mapping the repo, codifying entry-point recipes (tools/nodes/integrations), rules, testing commands, and the new-integration checklist.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured for LLM agent consumption; encodes if-X-then-Y rules and a numbered repo map.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Single-line indirection that imports AGENTS.md and CLAUDE_PERSONAL.md so Claude Code inherits the same instructions as other agents.

nodes.name: DEPLOYMENT.md
nodes.summary: Operator-facing guide describing how to deploy opensre to LangGraph Platform and Railway, including environment, build, and verification steps.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Production container image — installs the agent via `pip install --no-cache-dir -c /api/constraints.txt /deps/agent` (NON-editable) and exposes the LangGraph runtime via LANGSERVE_GRAPHS pointing at app/graph_pipeline.py:build_graph.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets LANGSERVE_GRAPHS to mount app/graph_pipeline.py:graph as the 'agent' graph; HEALTHCHECK uses /ok endpoint with 30s interval.

nodes.name: langgraph-api
nodes.summary: Single-stage container service built on langchain/langgraph-api:3.11 that publishes the opensre LangGraph agent on TCP 2024.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Canonical task runner exposing 96 targets covering install/build, lint/format/typecheck, test suites (cov, rca, synthetic, k8s, chaos), demos, integrations verification, and LangGraph deployment.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes phony build targets with helper targets (check-docker, check-langsmith-api-key) used as prerequisites; many targets shell out to make-x scripts and pytest selectors.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project overview covering motivation, install, quick start, deployment options, capabilities, supported integrations, benchmark results, contribution, security, and telemetry.

nodes.name: SETUP.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step environment setup walkthrough covering Python prerequisites, dependency install, env vars, integration credentials, and first-run verification.

nodes.name: install.ps1
nodes.summary: Windows PowerShell installer that resolves the opensre release archive on GitHub, verifies its SHA-256 checksum, extracts the binary, places it in ~/.local/bin, and updates the user PATH.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Set-StrictMode Latest, retry-with-backoff helper, and channel switch (release|main) to support stable vs nightly artifacts.

nodes.name: Invoke-OpenSreWithRetry
nodes.summary: Generic retry-with-backoff wrapper that re-invokes a script block up to MaxAttempts on transient errors while bailing fast on 4xx HTTP responses.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreHttpStatusCodeFromError
nodes.summary: Extracts an HTTP status code from a PowerShell ErrorRecord across both WebRequest and HttpResponseException shapes for cross-version compatibility.

nodes.name: Enable-OpenSreTls
nodes.summary: Forces ServicePointManager to use TLS 1.2/1.3 so older Windows PowerShell hosts can still reach api.github.com.

nodes.name: Invoke-OpenSreRestMethod
nodes.summary: Wraps Invoke-RestMethod with retry, headers, and TLS configuration for fetching JSON release metadata from GitHub.

nodes.name: Invoke-OpenSreWebRequest
nodes.summary: Wraps Invoke-WebRequest for binary asset downloads with retry support and consistent header/TLS configuration.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreRuntimeArchitecture
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical CPU architecture name (x86_64/arm64) by querying the .NET runtime information.

nodes.name: Resolve-OpenSreWindowsArchitecture
nodes.summary: Maps the Windows process architecture and PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE env var into the GitHub release naming scheme used by opensre artifacts.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreArchiveName
nodes.summary: Builds the expected GitHub release archive filename (zip) from the resolved tag and architecture for Windows targets.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreReleaseMetadata
nodes.summary: Fetches release metadata from the GitHub API (latest, by-tag, or main-channel branches), retrying on transient failures and returning a normalized record.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreReleaseAsset
nodes.summary: Locates a named asset on a release object and returns its browser_download_url, raising a clear error when missing.

nodes.name: Resolve-OpenSreArchiveDownload
nodes.summary: Coordinates resolution of the correct release archive across release/main channels with optional fallback when an architecture-specific asset is unavailable.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreExpectedSha256
nodes.summary: Downloads the SHA-256 checksum file alongside the archive and returns the expected hash for the artifact to verify against.

nodes.name: Normalize-OpenSrePath
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes filesystem paths so equality checks against the user PATH env var ignore casing and trailing separators.

nodes.name: Test-OpenSreDirectoryOnPath
nodes.summary: Determines whether a candidate directory already appears on the user's PATH so the installer can avoid duplicate entries.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreBinaryPathFromArchive
nodes.summary: Resolves the absolute path to the opensre.exe binary inside the extracted archive directory, handling nested release layouts.

nodes.name: Get-OpenSreBinaryVersionInfo
nodes.summary: Runs the freshly installed binary with --version (and friends) to surface a post-install confirmation banner to the user.

nodes.name: Install-OpenSre
nodes.summary: Top-level installer driver that resolves the release, downloads and verifies the archive, extracts and copies the binary into the install dir, and updates the user PATH.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: Cross-platform Bash installer that resolves the opensre GitHub release for the host triple, validates SHA-256, extracts the binary into a writable bin dir, and configures shell PATH.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -e/-u, helper functions for download_to/verify_checksum, and falls back across curl/wget plus tar/unzip; supports privileged install with sudo when needed.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints CLI help text describing the installer's flags (install dir, channel, version, prefix overrides).

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Long/short option parser populating installer globals (channel, install dir, version, force) from the command line.

nodes.name: fetch_release_json
nodes.summary: Calls the GitHub releases REST API and returns the JSON body, branching on whether curl or wget is available locally.

nodes.name: build_archive_name
nodes.summary: Constructs the expected release archive filename from the resolved tag, OS, and architecture triple (e.g. opensre-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz).

nodes.name: is_candidate_dir_writable
nodes.summary: Tests whether a candidate install directory exists and is writable by the current user without elevation.

nodes.name: select_writable_path_candidate_from_list
nodes.summary: Picks the first writable directory from a preference list of candidate bin dirs (e.g. ~/.local/bin, /usr/local/bin).

nodes.name: select_path_candidate_for_sudo
nodes.summary: Selects an appropriate system-wide install dir when the script will run privileged via sudo, preferring locations on the default PATH.

nodes.name: resolve_install_dir
nodes.summary: Resolves the final install directory by combining user override, writable candidates, and sudo fallback into a single chosen path.

nodes.name: to_windows_path
nodes.summary: Converts a POSIX path to the Windows-style equivalent for invoking PowerShell from MSYS/Cygwin shells.

nodes.name: extract_zip
nodes.summary: Extracts a ZIP archive using unzip when present and falling back to PowerShell Expand-Archive on Windows hosts.

nodes.name: extract_archive
nodes.summary: Dispatches archive extraction to extract_zip or tar based on the file extension of the downloaded asset.

nodes.name: verify_checksum
nodes.summary: Downloads the .sha256 sidecar and validates the archive hash with sha256sum/shasum/openssl, refusing to install on mismatch.

nodes.name: install_binary
nodes.summary: Copies the resolved opensre binary into the chosen install directory, optionally elevating with run_with_privilege.

nodes.name: get_binary_path_from_archive
nodes.summary: Searches the extracted archive for the opensre executable and returns its absolute path, handling nested release layouts.

nodes.name: verify_binary_version
nodes.summary: Runs the just-installed binary with --version to confirm the install succeeded and prints the resolved version banner.

nodes.name: configure_path
nodes.summary: Detects the user's shell rc file (bashrc/zshrc/fish/profile) and appends an export line for the install dir if it is missing.

nodes.name: langgraph.json
nodes.summary: LangGraph deployment manifest — declares `auth.path`, `http.app`, `image_distro`, and the `env` file plus the graph entry under `graphs`. Consumed by LangGraph Platform / langgraph deploy.

nodes.name: mypy.ini
nodes.summary: mypy configuration — strict typing baseline plus per-module overrides for third-party libraries that lack type stubs (boto3/botocore/kubernetes/requests/questionary).

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Canonical Python project manifest declaring the opensre package metadata, runtime/dev dependencies, console scripts, and tool configurations.

nodes.name: pytest.ini
nodes.summary: Pytest runner configuration registering markers, test paths, and warning filters for the opensre suite.

nodes.name: ruff.toml
nodes.summary: Ruff linter and formatter configuration controlling line length, enabled rule sets, and per-path ignores for the codebase.

nodes.name: streamable_http_client
nodes.summary: Async context-managed compatibility wrapper that forwards Streamable HTTP MCP transport calls to the current ``mcp`` SDK's ``streamable_http_client``, accepting and ignoring extra legacy kwargs (headers/timeout/sse_read_timeout) so older call sites keep working without rebinding errors.

nodes.name: get_available_actions
nodes.summary: Return the list of investigation-surface RegisteredTool entries by delegating to ``get_registered_tools('investigation')``; thin facade exposing the investigation slice of the auto-discovered tool registry.

nodes.name: extract_use_cases
nodes.summary: Parse a docstring's 'Useful for:' section with a regex and return the bullet items as a list of strings; used by the investigation registry to surface tool use-cases to the planner.

nodes.name: route_by_mode
nodes.summary: Top-level mode router for the unified chat/investigation graph; returns the next-node label based on AgentState mode.

nodes.name: route_chat
nodes.summary: Routes within chat mode; returns 'tools' when the model produced tool_calls, otherwise 'end'.

nodes.name: route_after_extract
nodes.summary: Routes after the extract_alert node; returns 'investigate' if extraction succeeded, otherwise terminal.

nodes.name: route_investigation_loop
nodes.summary: Loop controller for the planner→action→diagnose cycle; decides continue/exit based on state.investigation_loop_count and verdict.

nodes.name: should_call_tools
nodes.summary: Tool-call gate for the chat mode; returns 'tools' if the latest AIMessage contains pending tool_calls, else 'end'.

nodes.name: merge_results_reducer
nodes.summary: LangGraph reducer for the parallel hypothesis_results channel; supports a __clear sentinel to reset and otherwise concatenates incoming lists.

nodes.name: SimpleAgent
nodes.summary: @dataclass agent wrapper that lazy-imports the compiled LangGraph and calls .invoke; re-exported by app/pipeline/__init__.py for the public agent API.

nodes.name: get_output_format
nodes.summary: Read OPENSRE_OUTPUT_FORMAT env var with default 'pretty'; controls renderer selection across the CLI.

nodes.name: get_tracker
nodes.summary: Process-level singleton accessor for the global ProgressTracker (reset=True forces a new instance).

nodes.name: reset_tracker
nodes.summary: Drop the global ProgressTracker singleton (used by tests and between runs).

nodes.name: debug_print
nodes.summary: Emit a dim debug line to stderr when OPENSRE_VERBOSE is set; used across nodes and tools.

nodes.name: has_llm_api_key
nodes.summary: Return True iff a non-empty value is found in env or keyring for the given env_var name.

nodes.name: delete_llm_api_key
nodes.summary: Remove the cached credential from the OS keyring; tolerant when keyring is disabled.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: MCP server entrypoint — invokes mcp.run() to launch the FastMCP stdio server exposing the run_rca tool.

nodes.name: save_remote_url
nodes.summary: Persist the active remote URL into the local wizard config (single-active-remote shape).

nodes.name: load_named_remotes
nodes.summary: Load the {name → url} dict of named remotes from the local wizard config.

nodes.name: load_active_remote_name
nodes.summary: Return the currently-active remote name set via set_active_remote, or None.

nodes.name: is_model_present
nodes.summary: GET /api/tags from the Ollama daemon and check whether the (normalized) model tag is already pulled.

nodes.name: inject_auth_node
nodes.summary: First-run LangGraph node — extracts auth context (org_id/user_id/email/thread_id/run_id) from the RunnableConfig and merges it into AgentState. Registered in pipeline/graph.py as the 'inject_auth' node.

nodes.name: node_diagnose_root_cause
nodes.summary: @traceable LangSmith-tracked LangGraph wrapper that delegates to diagnose_root_cause(state). This is the function registered in pipeline/graph.py as the 'diagnose' node.

nodes.name: clear_tool_registry_cache
nodes.summary: Drop the cached registry snapshot — used by tests to force re-discovery via _iter_tool_module_names + pkgutil.iter_modules.

nodes.name: RemoteOpsError
nodes.summary: Base RuntimeError raised by remote-ops provider operations; imported by app/cli/commands/remote.py for user-facing error translation.

nodes.name: RemoteServiceScope
nodes.summary: @dataclass scope descriptor (provider/service/environment) — required argument on every RemoteOpsProvider method.

nodes.name: VercelResolutionError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError raised when Vercel project/URL/integration resolution fails; translated to HTTP 400 by remote/server.py investigate-stream handlers.

nodes.name: _check_disk_health
nodes.summary: Third leg of the /health/deep triplet (LLM + memory + disk) — returns DeepHealthCheck for filesystem availability.

nodes.name: get_grafana_client
nodes.summary: Env-based GrafanaClient factory — resolves credentials from env vars (the canonical entry point used outside the credential-store flow).

nodes.name: extract_org_id_from_jwt
nodes.summary: Decode an unverified JWT and return its org_id claim (used by tracer_client to propagate org context across remote calls).

nodes.name: get_guardrail_engine
nodes.summary: Module-level singleton accessor for the default GuardrailEngine; consumers import this rather than the class directly.

nodes.name: reset_guardrail_engine
nodes.summary: Drop the cached GuardrailEngine singleton; used by tests and config-reload paths.

nodes.name: is_railway_cli_installed
nodes.summary: Detect whether the Railway CLI is on PATH; precondition for the railway deploy method.

nodes.name: is_langgraph_cli_installed
nodes.summary: Detect whether the LangGraph CLI is on PATH; precondition for the langsmith deploy method.

nodes.name: extract_deployment_url
nodes.summary: Parse stdout from `langgraph deploy` to extract the resulting deployment URL.

nodes.name: get_remote_outputs_path
nodes.summary: Return the local path where EC2 deployment-output files are persisted.

nodes.name: delete_remote_outputs
nodes.summary: Delete the persisted EC2 deployment-output files (cleanup helper called from the CLI on `opensre deploy clean`).

nodes.name: Code of Conduct
nodes.summary: Contributor Covenant code of conduct for the OpenSRE project — community behavior expectations and reporting flow.

nodes.name: Security Policy
nodes.summary: Security disclosure policy — how to report vulnerabilities privately to the OpenSRE maintainers (do not open public issues for security).

nodes.name: Contributing Guide
nodes.summary: Contributor onboarding guide — paths for bugs/features/improvements, environment setup, development workflow, PR template, and code-quality standards. Now also points contributors to the .understand-anything/ codebase map.

nodes.name: AWSStaticCredentials
nodes.summary: StrictConfigModel for static AWS credentials (access_key_id, secret_access_key, session_token) — embedded in AWSIntegrationConfig.credentials when role assumption is not used.

nodes.name: _verify_with_validation_result
nodes.summary: Generic helper that runs a validate_*_config callable and turns the ValidationResult into the verifier evidence dict — the canonical wrapper used by build_validation_verifier.

nodes.name: _verify_discord
nodes.summary: Probe Discord webhook validity for the discord delivery integration.

nodes.name: _verify_telegram
nodes.summary: Probe Telegram bot token validity by calling getMe.

nodes.name: _verify_snowflake
nodes.summary: Probe Snowflake connectivity using the configured account/user/role.

nodes.name: _verify_azure
nodes.summary: Probe Azure Monitor connectivity (subscription + workspace lookup).

nodes.name: _verify_openobserve
nodes.summary: Probe OpenObserve API connectivity using the configured endpoint and auth.

nodes.name: _verify_opensearch
nodes.summary: Probe OpenSearch cluster reachability and auth.

nodes.name: _verify_slack_without_test
nodes.summary: Slack verifier variant that skips test-message posting (used during onboarding when permissions to post are not yet granted).

layers.name: CLI Surface
layers.description: User-facing Click CLI, onboarding wizard, interactive TTY shell, investigation runner, local-LLM helpers and command-support utilities under app/cli/.

layers.name: Runtime Core
layers.description: Application composition root: main.py, webapp.py, config/strict_config, llm_credentials, output, version, the LangGraph entry shim, plus shared state, types, constants and SDK/MCP entrypoints.

layers.name: LangGraph Nodes
layers.description: Per-stage LangGraph nodes orchestrating the investigation pipeline: alert extraction, window adaptation, planning, action execution, root-cause diagnosis, parallel execution, post-processing and publishing.

layers.name: Agent Tools
layers.description: Auto-discovered tool registry and per-tool packages exposed to the agent (Datadog, Grafana, GitHub, S3, Lambda, Sentry, Vercel, Kubernetes, etc.) plus shared tool decorators, base classes and helpers.

layers.name: Integration Layer
layers.description: Vendor integration configuration, normalization, validation, verification, selectors and the catalog/store, plus subprocess-backed LLM CLI adapters under app/integrations/llm_cli/.

layers.name: Vendor Service Clients
layers.description: Reusable HTTP/API clients and adapters used by tools and nodes (Datadog, Grafana, AlertManager, ArgoCD, Coralogix, EKS, LLM client, etc.), plus auth (JWT/JWKS), sandboxed runner, alert normalization and PostHog analytics.

layers.name: Cross-Cutting Concerns
layers.description: Utilities, delivery channels (Slack/Discord/Telegram), env/sentry/OTLP setup and truncation helpers, sensitive-data masking, and the guardrails engine/audit/rules used across the agent.

layers.name: Remote Hosted Runtime
layers.description: Hosted runtime surface: FastAPI server, streaming, Vercel poller, Slack/Discord context handlers and ops provider hooks under app/remote/.

layers.name: Deployment & Infrastructure
layers.description: Deployment methods (Railway, LangSmith) and runtime operations (health, EC2 config, provider validation), root-level Dockerfiles, install scripts, Makefile, plus docker-compose stacks and the install-proxy worker under infra/.

layers.name: Project Meta & CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows, issue/PR templates, dependabot/CodeQL configs, root-level project config (pyproject.toml, mypy.ini, pytest.ini, ruff.toml, langgraph.json, .env.example), top-level docs (README, AGENTS, CLAUDE, SETUP, DEPLOYMENT) and knowledge-graph metadata.

tour.title: What OpenSRE Is
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand the project's mission: an open-source framework for AI SRE agents that connect 60+ observability tools, run LangGraph-driven investigations, and ship scored synthetic RCA scenarios. AGENTS.md is the repo's contributor map and codifies the entry-point recipes (tools, nodes, integrations) you'll use later in the tour. Read these two together to align the rest of the tour with the project's own narrative before opening any code.

tour.title: The Process Entry Point
tour.description: app/main.py is the canonical entry point: it parses CLI args, builds (or loads) an alert payload, and dispatches into the synchronous run-investigation flow. It is the BFS root of the codebase — every dependency you'll see in the next steps is reachable from here. The Click-based CLI group lives next door in app/cli/__main__.py and routes subcommands like `opensre investigate` and `opensre wizard` to the same pipeline.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `if __name__ == '__main__'` idiom and the `__main__.py` file work together: a directory containing `__main__.py` becomes runnable via `python -m app.cli`, which is how the CLI is invoked in tests, in Docker, and as the `opensre` console script declared in pyproject.toml.

tour.title: The LangGraph Topology
tour.description: app/pipeline/graph.py builds the canonical LangGraph state machine — wiring auth, chat, alert extraction, parallel investigation, merge, evaluation, and publishing into the runtime agent. routing.py holds the predicates that decide chat vs investigation mode, when to fan out hypotheses, and when to terminate the loop. runners.py compiles the graph and translates LangGraph's stream events into the project's StreamEvent format. This trio is the heart of the agent: every node you meet later plugs into this topology.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph models an agent as a directed graph of nodes that mutate a shared TypedDict state. Conditional edges (driven by `routing.py` predicates) enable loops and fan-out — the planner can re-enter itself, and a single node can spawn N parallel investigation branches that the merge node then folds back together.

tour.title: The Shared State That Flows Through The Graph
tour.description: Every LangGraph node reads from and writes to a single shared `AgentState` TypedDict defined in agent_state.py — alerts, evidence, hypotheses, remediation actions, chat history. factory.py builds the canonical initial state for an investigation or chat run (including alert normalization and OpenRCA scoring extraction), and types.py defines the chat-message shapes. Understanding this state shape is the fastest way to reason about what any node does: each node is just a typed transform on this dict.
tour.languageLesson: TypedDict + a parallel Pydantic model is a common LangGraph pattern: the TypedDict gives you cheap, structural typing for the graph runtime (no per-step validation overhead), while the Pydantic twin is used at boundaries (CLI input, API ingest) to coerce and validate before the data enters the graph.

tour.title: Anatomy of a Node — Extract Alert
tour.description: extract_alert is the simplest node and a perfect template for understanding the pattern. The package barrel exposes node_extract_alert (the graph entry) and extract_alert_details (the underlying helper); models.py defines the strict Pydantic input/output contract for the LLM call; extract.py implements the core logic with a deterministic fallback when the LLM is unavailable; extract_node.py is the thin LangGraph wrapper that enriches the alert and emits a state delta. Every other node (adapt_window, plan_actions, root_cause_diagnosis, publish_findings) follows this same shape: barrel, models, pure logic, graph wrapper.

tour.title: The Investigation Loop — Plan, Execute, Diagnose
tour.description: These four nodes are the agentic core of OpenSRE. plan_actions selects the next batch of tool calls by combining source detection, keyword extraction, and an LLM planner. investigate/parallel.py runs each hypothesis branch concurrently, looking up available actions in the investigation registry and executing them. root_cause_diagnosis drives the final LLM judgment with claim validation and short-circuits for healthy systems or insufficient evidence. routing.py (revisited from Step 3) decides when to loop back to planning vs commit to a diagnosis. This is the loop that turns 'an alert' into 'a root cause'.

tour.title: Publishing The Findings
tour.description: publish_findings is the terminal node: it generates the final investigation report and dispatches it to delivery channels — Slack, Discord, Telegram, GitLab MR notes, the terminal, an editor, or an ingest endpoint. This is also where you'll see the project's pattern for keeping side-effecting code (HTTP posts, file writes, terminal printing) isolated behind delivery transports so the upstream graph stays pure. Pair this with the README's screenshots of the published reports to see the user-visible output the rest of the pipeline is feeding.

tour.title: The Tool Registry — How 60+ Tools Are Discovered
tour.description: tool_decorator.py is by far the most-imported file in the codebase (fan-in of 107) — every tool either subclasses BaseTool from base.py or is annotated with `@tool(...)` from this decorator. registry.py walks the app.tools package via pkgutil at startup, harvests every RegisteredTool, and exposes cached lookups by surface (investigation/chat). The DataDogLogsTool is a representative concrete example: a tiny module that declares its schema and source, reuses a shared Datadog client, and is auto-discovered with no manual registration. Adding a tool means dropping a module under app/tools/ and writing tests — that's the entire ceremony.
tour.languageLesson: The `@tool` decorator pattern + pkgutil walking is Python's answer to compile-time annotation processors: tools register themselves by simply being imported, and a single registry lookup at startup builds the catalog. This keeps per-tool boilerplate near zero and means you never edit a central list when adding a tool.

tour.title: Integrations — From Env Vars to a Live Tool
tour.description: An integration is the configuration layer beneath a tool. _catalog_impl.py classifies integrations by service and parses per-instance env vars across ~30 vendors; config_models.py holds strict Pydantic models for every supported vendor (Grafana, Datadog, AWS, Slack, OpsGenie, databases, chat); store.py is a JSON-backed persistent store with v1->v2 schema migration; verify.py orchestrates per-service health probes, with probes.py defining the canonical ProbeResult shape. The Datadog client in services/ is the actual HTTP layer that the DataDogLogsTool from Step 8 calls. Trace one integration top-to-bottom and the rest of the 60+ become formulaic.

tour.title: The Onboarding Wizard
tour.description: When a new user runs `opensre` for the first time, the wizard at app/cli/wizard/ takes over. flow.py orchestrates the questionary-driven prompts: select integrations, capture credentials, validate them against the actual remote APIs, and persist the result via the integrations store from Step 9. cli/investigation/investigate.py is the bridge that takes the resolved alert context and runs the LangGraph runner from Step 3 in either sync or streaming mode. This is how a brand-new user goes from `pip install opensre` to a fully-configured agent investigating a real alert in under five minutes.

tour.title: The Hosted Runtime — FastAPI, LangGraph Platform, Docker
tour.description: OpenSRE also runs as a hosted service. webapp.py is a FastAPI app exposing /health (liveness, LLM-config validity, version). remote/stream.py parses Server-Sent Events from the hosted runtime back into the same StreamEvent shape the CLI uses. langgraph.json declares the LangGraph Platform entry, and the Dockerfile builds on langchain/langgraph-api:3.11, copies the agent into /deps/agent, installs in editable mode, exposes 2024, and adds a /ok healthcheck. DEPLOYMENT.md is the operator-facing companion that ties it all together for Railway and LangGraph Platform.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph Platform deployments are declarative: langgraph.json points at a graph factory function, and the runtime handles persistence, streaming, and concurrency. The Dockerfile uses editable install (`pip install -e`) instead of a multi-stage build because the LangGraph API base image already provides the runtime — you're shipping just your graph code.

tour.title: Quality Gates — Tests, CI, Make
tour.description: Makefile is the canonical task runner with 96 targets covering install, lint/format/typecheck, test suites (cov, rca, synthetic, k8s, chaos), demos, integration verification, and LangGraph deployment — `make check` is the one-shot quality gate. The CI workflow runs lint, format, type-check, unit, and Kubernetes integration jobs on every push. The synthetic-tests workflow is OpenSRE's signature: the scored synthetic RCA scenarios run on every PR, guarding against regressions in the agent's reasoning quality (analogous to SWE-bench for coding). pyproject.toml ties dependencies, console scripts, and tooling configuration together. With this, you have everything needed to add a tool, add a node, or add an integration following the AGENTS.md recipes from Step 1.
